Hi各位連登巴絲，第一次開post
我係Adrià Ballester，係加泰嘅社運人士。


我由2019年開始就關注香港嘅革命，之前仲去過中國駐巴塞領事館嗌抗議，可以睇返呢個post：

加泰手足因為聲援港人，被國安跟蹤同騷擾！
https://lih.kg/2151553
- 分享自 LIHKG 討論區

自此我social media收到大量香港手足嘅支持，於是開咗個連登acc，想同香港手足多啲交流


其實我由2012年開始就係加泰隆尼亞嘅前線抗爭者，試過參與好多危險嘅示威活動，亦都多次被捕。希望我嘅經驗可以幫助到香港手足。

如果大家關於加泰/香港有咩想問我，我好樂意解答！

Finally see you here and pick that username elected on Twitter!

I got 3 questions and I find them interesting. See if you can tell us more to understand another side of Catalunya.

1. You may know hkers are now debating whether the parliamentarians should accept to form a temporary parliament by Chinazi. Besides hkers have not authorised elected parliamentarians to accept the offer from the govt, do you think the parliament can still function in the future, based on the experience of Catalan’s protests and Puigdemont’s leave?

2. Following the above, how catalans see Puidgemont as a leader of Catalan Independence Movement?

3. Some people said Catalans support communism and even waved Chinazi’s flag on the streets during the protest last year in Catalunya. Is it true that Catalans really uphold the values of communism? I don’t think so though.

I hope lihkg bro and sis and learn some insights from Catalan sau juk. Maybe we can stop arguing whether to support the parliamentarians to remain in the legco.

By the way, there are some hostile users who are afraid of connecting Catalans and sometimes defame them. Just treat them like douchebags. LIHKG welcomes you as our new member.

replies.msg:
That is a really interesting question. The most important thing is to remember that no matter what happens with this topic we still stand for freedom. Both options have advantages and disadvantages and no matter if you think we should form a temporary parliament or not we need to understand that all the yellow team stands for freedom. In Catalonia we got divided by "accepting spanish punishments or not" and that was our mistake. We should not judge people by their opinion about how to achieve freedom but we should judge them by if they want to achieve freedom or not



2. in Catalonia there are 2 main parties for independence, both of them are quite opposite ideologically and both of them are the same size. I would say 75% think Puigdemont is a great leader who risked everything for Catalonia freedom and 25% think he shouldn't have gone

3. Some young people wear communist flags, no china ones. Just some but I would be lying if I say it is impossible. Please take into account that Spain was a "right-wing fascist dictatorship" and people with communist ideas came to fight this dictatorship, so the youngsters have some communist flags as a protest for our previous dictatorship but not because they really engage with communist ideas. It would be similar to HK people wearing USA flags
